name: The Go Engineer v3 — README Contracts

architectural_decision.decision: Break the locked v2 curriculum and rebuild around the Report 1 phase-based zero-to-software-engineer path.
architectural_decision.v2_policy: curriculum.v2.json is frozen legacy import data, not the active source of truth.
architectural_decision.core_rule: Keep good lessons, break the old path, optimize for learner progression, proof, documentation quality, and job readiness.
architectural_decision.full_bundle_policy: Split files are authoritative. curriculum.v3.full.generated.json is an optional generated snapshot for review/export only.

contracts.purpose: Learner front door and curriculum overview with explicit repository layout and proof expectations.
required_headings: What you will become able to do
What you will be able to do

contracts.purpose: World-class teaching README for a concept or skill.
contracts.applies_when: The concept is required for the core path and every learner must master it before advancing.

status_levels.placeholder: Heading scaffold only. No substantive content.
status_levels.draft: Content written but not reviewed for quality or production realism.
status_levels.reviewed: Content reviewed by a senior engineer for technical accuracy.
status_levels.golden: Content passes all validator quality rules and has been tested with learners.

zero_magic_enforcement.promotion_policy: A lesson may only be promoted to golden after: (1) passing validate-no-placeholder-zm with semantic checks enabled, (2) being reviewed by a senior engineer, (3) containing a real production example and real debugging scenario.
zero_magic_enforcement.regression_policy: Any lesson that fails a semantic quality check must be immediately reverted to draft status regardless of previous promotion.

contracts.purpose: Advanced reference/tutorial hybrid that does not block the core path.
required_headings: When you do not need this

contracts.purpose: Project README with build requirements, verification, and rubric.
Assessment README with evidence and passing criteria.
Opslane learner front door and graduation proof map.

validation_rules: Every README must match its declared contract_id.
Generated sections must be marked with curriculum generated comments.
Human-written sections must not be overwritten by generators.
No implemented lesson may keep placeholder zero-magic text.
No README may use stale v2-only navigation labels unless inside migration notes.
Every future concept must use a preview-only reference.
Every project README must expose its rubric.
Every module README must expose its exit checklist.
No README with zero_magic_status=golden may contain template-style phrases: 'Think of X as a contract', 'X is similar to', 'in simple terms', 'basically', 'just', or empty analogy filler.
Every lesson README marked golden must contain at least one concrete production failure example in the 'In production' section.
Every lesson README marked golden must include a realistic debugging scenario in 'Debugging task', not a generic 'fix the bug' placeholder.
Mental model sections must describe the mechanism, not the definition — 'A goroutine is...' is a definition; 'When you write go f(), the runtime...' is a mental model.
Machine view sections must describe what the CPU/OS/runtime actually does, not restate the mental model.
Common mistakes sections must list real mistakes observed in production code, not conceptual misunderstandings that beginners never actually make.
No README may ship with TODO, TBD, placeholder, lorem ipsum, or scaffold-only language.
README paths must follow modules/<module>/<lesson>/README.md or electives/advanced-electives/<lesson>/README.md.
